fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.helpText: Generate at github.com/settings/tokens with required scopes

healthcheck_config.description: Validates PAT via GitHub user endpoint

metadata.summary: GitHub for repositories, issues, pull requests, and CI/CD.
metadata.setup_guide:
1. Go to github.com/settings/tokens?type=beta for fine-grained tokens
2. Click 'Generate new token', set expiration and repository access
3. Select required permissions: Contents (read), Issues (read/write), Pull requests (read/write)
4. Click 'Generate token' and copy the github_pat_... value

llm_usage_hint.overview: GitHub REST API v3. Auth via PAT in $GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN. Use curl with 'Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN' and 'Accept: application/vnd.github+json'. Base URL: https://api.github.com.
gotchas: Pagination defaults to 30 items; use ?per_page=100 (max 100). Follow Link header for next pages.
Rate limit is 5000/hr authenticated. Check X-RateLimit-Remaining response header.
Issues and pull requests share an ID namespace -- a PR is also an issue, but not vice versa. Use /issues/{n} for both, /pulls/{n} only for PRs.
Fine-grained PATs have per-repository scopes; a 404 may actually mean 'no permission' rather than 'not found'.
